item.title: 5.6 lakh litres of fuel seized
item.description: The government has seized more than 5.62 lakh litres of fuel during more than 10,500 anti-hoarding mobile court raids across the country....

item.title: Govt to import 1.5 lakh tonnes of diesel, 25,000 tonnes of octane
item.description: The cabinet committee on government purchase on Tuesday at a meeting approved the procurement of 1.5 lakh tonnes of diesel and 25,000 tonnes of octane without any tender amid a supply disruption of fuel oils due to the war in the Gulf region......

item.title: Legal basis of consensus commission questioned in JS
item.description: Bangladesh Nationalist Party lawmaker for Lakshmipur-1 Shahadat Hossain Selim on Tuesday questioned in the Jatiya Sangsad the legal basis of the National Consensus Commission formed by the interim government, alleging that its activities......

item.title: Fuel oil crisis passes 46 days
item.description: The overall mismanagement in supplying fuel oils to toiling consumers at few overcrowded pumps under the scorching sun extended for another day on Tuesday....

item.title: Interim govt restricted freedom of expression, association: Amnesty
item.description: International rights organisation Amnesty International said on Tuesday that Bangladesh’s interim government unnecessarily restricted freedom of expression, association and assembly, as individuals, including human rights defenders, were subjected......

item.title: NOAB seeks PM’s support on ad dues, cases against journalists
item.description: The Newspaper Owners’ Association of Bangladesh on Tuesday sought the support of prime minister Tarique Rahman to address longstanding challenges facing the media industry, including the release of substantial government unpaid......

item.title: Warrant issued for arrest of lawmaker Amir Hamza
item.description: The Chauhali Cognisance Court in Sirajganj on Tuesday issued an arrest warrant against Kushtia-3 lawmaker and Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami leader Mufti Amir Hamza in a defamation case filed over his alleged derogatory remarks about power, energy and mineral resources affairs minister......

item.title: No cases filed in 61pc attacks on shrines, darbars
item.description: Attacks, including vandalism, looting and arson, on shrines and darbars have continued across the country since August 2024 as no cases have been filed in 61 per cent of such incidents during the time. ...

item.title: Rethinking SSC exams
item.description: THIS year, more than 1.8 million students are sitting for the Secondary School Certificate and equivalent examinations. The examinations shape not only academic pathways but also the emotional landscape of an entire generation. For decades, the examinations have been treated as a gateway to higher secondary education and beyond. Yet, from policy and pedagogical......

item.title: Growing threats of yaba economy
item.description: LET’S imagine a scenario of a fishing trawler leaving Chattogram harbour before sunrise. The nets are folded, the crew is drowsy, and everything looks like an ordinary morning of honest work. But hidden in the hold are 500,000 yaba tablets, seized in April 2026 by the Rapid Action Battalion. That very same morning, Border Guard Bangladesh confiscated over a million......

item.title: A delta on the edge of climate strain
item.description: AS THE first light of International Mother Earth Day 2026 breaks over the emerald canopy of the Sundarbans and the rolling hills of the Chittagong Hill Tracts, Bangladesh stands as a stark symbol of both vulnerability and resilience. For a country shaped by rivers, forests, and fragile coastlines, the call to ‘Our Power, Our Planet’ is not a ceremonial slogan. It reflects an......

item.title: Exporting agri-entrepreneurship
item.description: BANGLADESH has successfully ‘exported’ agricultural labour for decades to Qatar and Malaysia, where our farmers have evolved from labourers into entrepreneurs. This article proposes a formal policy shift — from unorganised labour migration to state-backed agricultural investment in English-speaking African nations such as Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya and Zambia. By......

item.title: Court expected to reconsider media access decision
item.description: JOURNALISTS being instructed to leave a courtroom before the hearing of a constitutional matter prompts concern about media access to judicial proceedings. A High Court bench on April 20 asked journalists to leave the courtroom when it heard a writ petition that challenged the Supreme Court Secretariat (Repeal) Act 2026 and sought the maintenance of a status quo on......

item.title: Govt must treat hospitals first to stop measles spread
item.description: THE exposure of children to measles inside hospitals where they have been admitted for unrelated illnesses lays bare a grave failure in infection control and patient safety. Reports from multiple hospitals show that children treated for conditions such as diarrhoea, bronchitis and kidney complications were placed in shared wards alongside suspected measles patients. Such......

item.title: Russian strikes wound 25 in Ukraine overnight
item.description: Russian strikes across Ukraine wounded more than a dozen people in at least four regions, at a time when US-led peace talks to end the war have ground to a halt, officials said Tuesday....

item.title: Predator leaders seek to impose new world order: Amnesty
item.description: The leaders of the United States, Israel and Russia are seeking to impose a new ‘predatory’ world order while most countries are too cowardly to stop them, rights group Amnesty International said Tuesday....

item.title: Polls campaign heats up in West Bengal
item.description: Campaigning is under way across West Bengal, India, with a war of words intensifying amongpoliticalparties, reports hindustantimes.com on Tuesday....

item.title: 7 held as cancer hospital deputy director hacked over ‘tender dispute’
item.description: The deputy director of the National Institute of Cancer Research and Hospital, Ahmed Hossain, was stabbed in the capital’s Mohakhali area on Monday by a group of miscreants in an attempt to intimidate him over a tender dispute, Rapid Action Battalion officials said on Tuesday....

item.title: Madrassah student dies as headscarf gets tangled in wheels
item.description: A higher-secondary-level madrassah student died at Ishwardi upazila in Pabna on Tuesday morning after her headscarf got entangled in the wheels of a CNG-run auto-rickshaw she was riding in....

item.title: 53 submit nominations for JS reserved women’s seats
item.description: Fifty three candidates submitted their nomination papers till 4:00pm on Tuesday for the election to the reserved women’s seats in the 13th Jatiya Sangsad, representing various political parties, alliances and as independents....

item.title: SC to hold virtual hearings on Wednesdays, Thursdays
item.description: The Supreme Court of Bangladesh will hold hearings virtually on Wednesdays and Thursdays to save energy and power, according to an official notice....

item.title: Meeting on bus fare adjustment today
item.description: The road transport and bridges ministry is scheduled to hold a meeting today to adjust the fares of the city service and long-route buses in the face of the recent hike in fuel prices....

item.title: Swechchhasebak Dal leader, 9 others injured in shooting
item.description: AT LEAST 10 people, including a local leader of the Jatiyatabadi Swechchhasebak Dal, the volunteer wing of the ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party, were injured when unidentified assailants opened fire at Daulatpur upazila in Kushtia on Monday night....

item.title: LNG outage cuts Dhaka gas supply by 400 MMCFD
item.description: Gas supply in Bangladesh dropped sharply by around 400 million cubic feet per day after one of the country’s two floating storage and regasification units went offline due to a technical fault, authorities said Tuesday....

item.title: Ctg marks World Water Day with focus on gender equality
item.description: World Water Day 2026 was observed in Chattogram with a focus on gender equality and inclusive water management, aiming to improve access to safe water for low-income urban communities....

item.title: Four siblings among 7 to die for killing student
item.description: A Dhaka court on Tuesday sentenced seven people, including four brothers, to death for killing a college student in Tarakanda upazila of Mymensingh in 2021....Geographic Reference

item.title: IAEA mission supports isotope-based water management
item.description: A Technical Cooperation Expert Mission of the International Atomic Energy Agency is underway in Bangladesh to support sustainable water resource management using isotope hydrology....

item.title: Newlywed woman found hanging in Kushtia
item.description: THE police recovered the hanging body of a newly married woman from her bedroom in Mirpur upazila of Kushtia on Monday night....

item.title: 3 JnU students accuse fellow student of sexual misconduct
item.description: Three female students at Jagannath University have filed formal complaints accusing a fellow student of sexual misconduct, according to university officials....

item.title: Rajshahi Nursing college students for shutdown demanding principal appointment
item.description: Students of Rajshahi Nursing College on Tuesday announced a ‘complete shutdown’ programme for an indefinite period demanding immediate appointment of a principal at the college....

item.title: UGC urges austerity, transparency in key edn projects
item.description: University Grants Commission chairman Professor Mamun Ahmed has called for avoiding unnecessary expenditure and ensuring transparency in the implementation of two major higher education projects....

item.title: Cancer awareness prog held in Rajbari school
item.description: A cancer awareness programme was held at Chowdhury Abdul Hamid Academy in Goalanda upazila of Rajbari on Tuesday, aiming to promote early detection and prevention among local communities....

item.title: GAU plans to integrate CABI courses into curriculum
item.description: Gazipur Agricultural University has moved to incorporate courses from the CABI Academy into its curriculum to make higher education more practical and globally aligned....

item.title: BMU seminar urges child mental health action plan
item.description: A seminar on child and adolescent mental health was held at Bangladesh Medical University on Tuesday, focusing on expanding services and improving care quality through a national action plan....

item.title: Political culture neglects people’s culture: discussion
item.description: Journalists, teachers and writers stated on Tuesday that the country’spoliticalculture has neglected the culture of the people and that governments have shown indifference to the cultural sector due to a lack of funding....

item.title: Inter-district bus service from Rajshahi stalled as operators feud
item.description: Bus communication between Rajshahi and the rest of the country was halted on Tuesday abruptly, as transport owners and workers enforced an indefinite strike without prior notice over an internal dispute, causing widespread suffering to travellers....

item.title: Bangladesh urges stronger regional cooperation at ESCAP session
item.description: Bangladesh called for enhanced regional cooperation to tackle climate change, economic pressures and global uncertainties at the 82nd session of the United Nations Economic and Social Commission for Asia and the Pacific in Bangkok on Tuesday....

item.title: Palak ordered submarine cable shutdown, witness tells ICT
item.description: A senior telecom official on Tuesday told the International Crimes Tribunal-1 that former state minister Junaid Ahmed Palak instructed him to shut down the internet at the submarine cable level during the 2024 mass uprising....

item.title: Chhatra Dal, Shibir clash at Ctg Govt City College
item.description: Leaders and activists of Bangladesh Jatiyatabadi Chhatra Dal and Bangladesh Islami Chhatra Shibir clashed at Government City College in Chattogram on Tuesday over the altering a graffiti slogan, leaving several people injured....

item.title: Interns go on strike as fellows harassed after patient’s death
item.description: Patients at Rajshahi Medical College Hospital suffered on Tuesday as intern doctors observed a 24-hour work-abstention programme, protesting at alleged harassment by patients’ relatives following a confrontation over a patient’s death....

item.title: SC ruling sought on judicial independence
item.description: Badiul Alam Majumdar, secretary of civil society platform Shushashoner Jonno Nagorik, has moved the Appellate Division seeking a final ruling on key constitutional questions related to judicial independence and the establishment of a separate Supreme Court Secretariat. ...

item.title: Seminar on AI transformation held at UIU
item.description: The Institutional Quality Assurance Cell of United International University organised a seminar on AI transformation on Monday on the campus at United City in Dhaka’s Madani Avenue....Geographic Reference

item.title: Global legal push launched to recover laundered assets: minister
item.description: Finance minister Amir Khasru Mahmud Chowdhury on Tuesday told the Jatiya Sangsad that the government had initiated international legal assistance procedures to recover assets allegedly laundered abroad by S Alam Group and Beximco Group....
